ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Civil Law Suit
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Continued To Dec-12-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

E. DAVID MARKS, Esq., via Zoom, for Abigail Levinson Marks, Executor.

At the court's query, Mr. Marks reports as to the status of the related lawsuit and that the estate is ready to close and expects that a petition should be filed soon. The court points to Counsel's failure to appear at the previous calling. Counsel reports of filing a response to the Order to Show Cause. The court acknowledges the filing and advises that the response did not include good cause. Counsel is ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $250 for failure to appear. The Order to Appear, etc., is off calendar.

The subject matter is continued to Dec-12-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cabrera. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Feb-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Marks appeared by zoom for Abigail Marks.

The Court noted that Abigail Marks was not present and informed Counsel that when it issues an order to appear to a specific person, that person is required to appear at the hearing. Mr. Marks stated that he substituted in last week and that he told Ms. Marks that he would appear on her behalf. The Court informed Mr. Marks that it needs a proposed order, that the petition needs to be served, that notice needs to be filed, and explained that there is a difference between a notice and a proof of service. Mr. Marks spoke of the challenges Ms. Marks is having with getting documents e-filed, and stated that he will get the documents filed and that he will make sure they are done correctly. Mr. Marks also stated that the law suit is not moving forward and that the only thing left is the litigation regarding the cell phone. The status report is continued to February 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Marks is to have his documents filed one week before the hearing. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Continued To Jan-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: No Appearnces

This matter comes on calendar for Status Report and Petition for Additional Time for Estate Administration. The Court indicates that this matter has been continued administratively a couple of times to get a proposed order and courtesy copies. To date, the Court has not heard from the Petitioner. Since the petition has been filed, counsel of record filed a Substitution of Attorney. Ms. Abigail Marks as the representative is now self-represented. The Court indicates that the issues presented in the tentative ruling or the notes have not been resolved and appearances are required. There having been no appearances on the matter, the Court issues an Order to Appear to Abigail Marks to explain why she is not present this date. Ms. Marks is also ordered to explain why the issues raised in the tentative ruling have not been resolved and to resolve the status report itself. The Order to Appear will be set for January 19, 2021 at 9:00 AM in Department 204. The status report and petition for additional time for state administration is also continued to January 19, 2021 at 9:00 AM in Department 204. Court stands adjourned.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Jones, Deputy: N. Ng. Matter Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Terese Raddie appeared by telephone for Abigail Marks.

Ms. Raddie stated that she was informed that the Plaintiff's expert depositions in the Washington case were completed in January, that the Defendant's will go through May, that they are waiting for a ruling from the Judge for a December hearing and that there is no trial date. Ms. Raddie also stated that this is a class action suit against a cell phone company alleging that they cause cancer, that the Decedent was one of the Plaintiffs, that if they receive funds from the litigation, it will go into the probate estate, that there is nothing to probate except for what comes out of litigation and requested an 18-month continuance. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration and Request for Additional Time to Administer Estate (subject to review of the order). Status on close of estate is set for April 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
